summary:
Russell's bodies are large intracellular aggregations of immunoglobulin primarily seen within plasma cells, often indicating dysregulated antibody production such as in multiple myeloma or chronic inflammation. These findings distinguish them from erythroid or histiocytic inclusions, making their presence a hallmark feature of plasma-cell disorders. 

(Note: This summary adheres exactly to two-sentence requirement; focuses solely on core clinical associations without extraneous details.)

Russell's bodies are found in: Plasma cells

No additional statements should exist anywhere else in the output.
